title: #[non_exhaustive] on both Error enums
description: Both wolftpm::Error and wolftpm_tss::Error are marked #[non_exhaustive]. This allows new error variants to be added in minor-version releases without breaking downstream match arms. TPM error handling is a space where new variants are likely: new transport types, new protocol-level error distinctions, new hardware error categories. Without non_exhaustive, any addition would require a semver major bump. Alternative rejected: making Error a struct with an opaque kind field avoids exhaustiveness entirely but loses the ability for callers to match specific variants cleanly. The non_exhaustive attribute is the standard Rust idiom for this tradeoff.
